scope: DART exact-FBF incline threshold cells at mu=0.4 and mu=0.5

source_claim.keyframe_note: The source frames were inspected from a temporary local copy and are not repository goldens or redistributed deliverables.

capture.command: out=docs/dev_tasks/fbf_exact_coulomb_friction/assets/paper_evidence/fig01_02_incline; args=(--scene fbf_paper_incline --headless --steps 120 --width 1280 --height 720 --threads 1 --headless-sidecar $out/timeline.json); for step in 0 30 60 90 120; do args+=(--headless-shot-at $step:$out/frames/frame_$(printf '%03d' $step).png); done; build/default/cpp/Release/bin/dart-demos ${args[@]}
capture.note: Both friction cells are rendered in the same world, camera, solver, and deterministic run.

visual_claims.claim: The mu=0.5 DART cube remains visually fixed through T=2 s.
visual_claims.support: The green-cube color centroid in the rendered-world region moves from x=756.90 px at step 0 to x=756.47 px at step 120, while the physical trace reports only 0.912 mm downhill displacement.

visual_claims.claim: The mu=0.4 DART cube slides downhill while maintaining the implemented contact response.
visual_claims.support: The orange cube moves across the five inspected frames and leaves the cropped camera region by T=2 s; the trace reports 1.76869 m downhill displacement, 3 terminal contacts, and zero fallbacks.

visual_claims.claim: The shown DART run used exact FBF without boxed-LCP fallback.
visual_claims.support: The one-world sidecar reports 240 exact solves, zero exact failures, and zero boxed-LCP fallbacks across the two cells.

composition.panel_preparation_command: for step in 0 30 60 90 120; do .pixi/envs/gazebo/bin/ffmpeg -y -i frames/frame_$(printf '%03d' $step).png -vf crop=660:506:260:58 -frames:v 1 panels/frame_$(printf '%03d' $step).png; done

video.note: This compact DART-only physical clip is not the paper video's 11-second three-solver edit.

image_verification.strict_sheet_contrast_note: The optional sheet contrast gate rejects the cropped composition because it has no pixels above its generic bright threshold; the failure is retained.

unmet_requirements: Figure 1 still lacks the full published mu sweep and a source-matched analytic/FBF/Kamino/MuJoCo plot.
Figure 2 and video.03 still lack synchronized Kamino and MuJoCo views, an approved source golden, and a golden diff.
The tracked DART plane fixture reports 3 contacts per cell while the paper timing table reports 4; this contact-frontend mismatch prevents a paper-equivalent claim.
The compact DART clip is 2.03 seconds and does not reproduce the paper video's 11-second title and three-solver edit.
